focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 4

By the time the deputy minister reached tariff reform for the third time, Anya had stopped pretending the forum might improve. The conference room on the third floor of the Foreign Affairs Ministry was too hot, the windows were sweating grey, and most of the audience had already given up on listening.

*...salmon looked better before they put the dill on it...*

*...if Dreher says "framework" again I'm leaving...*

*...Marta will kill me if I don't text before six...*

Then one thought cut straight through the mush.

*...security meeting again. Not on the invite list. Who cleared Desmond for that?*

Damian was near the front, just left of the podium, speaking to two ministry men in the careful public tone he used in government rooms. Dark suit. Straight back. One hand loose at his side, the other around a folder he probably did not need. His thoughts, when she brushed them, were neat and annoyingly disciplined.

*...border checkpoint issue, keep it procedural...*

*...pick up dry cleaning before Friday...*

The suspicious thought was not his. It belonged to the blond man beside him, tall and thinning and dressed one salary bracket above what his badge suggested he should manage.

*...naval attaché, depot questions, procurement office... not random. Pattern.*

Anya shifted a step to the right until she could read the badge when he turned.

BRANDT, K. Ministry of the Interior, Policy Liaison.

Brandt was still looking at Damian when the next fragment hit.

*...Gessler tonight. He'll want the Desmond boy looked at.*

Gessler meant nothing to her, but Brandt had laid the name right beside depot questions, procurement offices, and Damian's face. He was not gossiping in his own head. He was stacking details for somebody above him. Maybe Renewal Front. Maybe one f...
open_hooks_to_carry: Anya files a misleading report to WISE, downgrading Damian's threat profile and minimizing Renewal Front intelligence
She reads Sylvia's complete trust during the debriefing — the specific pain of watching someone believe your lie while you read the trust that makes it possible
The false report is a chapter-level event, not a transition between scenes — dramatize the weight of this institutional betrayal
Cultural exchange event: Damian's partial recognition — something about her face, her laugh
The dual-layer conversation: spoken words vs. Damian's unfiltered thoughts
Comic irony: Anya managing her reactions to his embarrassing/flattering thoughts
Anya pushing the connection past protocol because of both strategic need and genuine interest
prev_tail_excerpt:
...r towel shoved at him.
A girl's face gone wide with horror.
That same girl's mouth twitching because the hit had been an accident and also, unfortunately, impressive.

Anya stopped breathing for one beat.

He kept turning the memory over in scraps.

Green eyes. Head tilted left. An apology that had not sounded sorry enough. Pink hair again, impossible to mistake.

And then the start of a name. Just the first sound. *An.*

The terrace door opened.

"Desmond," a man in a grey suit called from inside. "The deputy minister wants you."

Damian straightened at once. The schoolyard vanished. His mind snapped back into neat political lines so fast it felt violent.

"Of course."

He picked up his glass and turned toward the doors. As he crossed past her, six feet away at most, his thoughts were already on appropriations, border posture, who hated whom at the deputy minister's table. Public face back on. Diplomat again.

One brief glance brushed over her. Automatic, polite, forgettable.

*Westalian. Freezing. Why don't any of them ever bring coats?*

Then he was through the doors and gone into the big hot noise of the ballroom.

He hadn't recognized her fully. All he had was pink hair, a punch, the front edge of her name.

Anya stayed on the terrace until the cold bit through the dress and into muscle. Her fingers ached around the dead champagne glass. Tomorrow she'd hand Sylvia a report built out of scraps and timing and leave out the part where she'd gone rifling through Damian Desmond's head. The lie was already sitting on her tongue, flat as the champagne.

Anya set the warm glass on the railing. Below, the Ministry gardens were dark blocks and pale gravel. Wind moved through the last leaves with a dry whisper.

She smoothed the front of her dress and went back into the heat.

setups_to_plant.description: Anya's misleading WISE assessment creates a traceable discrepancy that will be exploited by the mole and contribute to operational failures
Establish Anya's growing guilt about the asymmetry — she negotiates with full access to thoughts while others negotiate blind; she lies while reading the trust that enables the lie

state_deltas_required: Anya must file an assessment to WISE about Damian while managing her conflicting knowledge and loyalties; then she must establish contact
Anya commits institutional betrayal by filing a false report — a traceable lie with unforeseen consequences. Contact with Damian is established. She now operates under two deceptions.
